mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 5.0% threshold, labor market slack triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity risk premiums and drive negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX forward 60-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals a synchronized collapse in consumer demand, which forces corporate earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a combined regression coefficient statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial production growth, supply-side momentum creates short-term liquidity inflows into equities, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during early-cycle recoveries, with a mean forward return below zero and a t-statistic less than 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Steady increases in total nonfarm payrolls expand aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, mechanically lifting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no positive correlation with 20-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation above the 0.4% monthly threshold forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy expectations, raising discount rates and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL spikes above 0.4% monthly fail to precede negative 60-day returns, with forward returns averaging positive values in >60% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real personal consumption expenditures indicates unsustainable economic expansion, triggering mean-reversion trades that produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric yields a positive or zero predictive coefficient for 60-day returns, failing to reject the null hypothesis of no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment regime, any further decline in the jobless rate signals labor market overheating and impending wage inflation, which compresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: UNRATE declines during full employment periods fail to predict negative 5-day returns, with a mean forward return above zero and a predictive R-squared below 0.05.
